content: We can reframe the vanilla Self-Refine framework to integrate multiple inconsistent self-perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a hierarchical self-refinement system where single-pass self-feedback is first diversified across conflicting solving perspectives before iterative refinement begins. This hybrid approach would retain Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no extra training or supervision while addressing its key limitation of getting stuck in local feedback loops caused by uniform single-perspective self-feedback.
